name: Alert on spend with HTTP / REST API and Slack end to end

parameters.text: =✅ Alert on spend with HTTP / REST API and Slack end to end — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Every Few Minutes

Starts the workflow on a timer — every 10 min. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Fetch Current Metrics

### Fetch Current Metrics

GET https://api.example.com/v1/events — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Next: Compute Severity Signals

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Compute Severity Signals

Custom JavaScript: Score each event 0-100 from simple heuristics

Next: Grade Severity Tier

### Grade Severity Tier

Routes each item to one of 4 lanes: info, warning, critical + fallback.

Ports: ①→ Just Record (Info) · ②→ Warn Channel (Slack) · ③→ Page Urgently (Slack) · ④→ Archive Unscored

### Just Record (Info)

Appends a new row to 'Event Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Next: Merge Tier Outcomes

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Warn Channel (Slack)

Posts a message to Slack (`#alerts`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Next: Merge Tier Outcomes

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Page Urgently (Slack)

Posts a message to Slack (`#alerts`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Next: Merge Tier Outcomes

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Archive Unscored

Appends a new row to 'Event Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Next: Merge Tier Outcomes

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Merge Tier Outcomes

Rejoins 4 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Send Summary to Slack

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#alerts`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Alert on spend with HTTP / REST API and Slack end to end

**Pattern:** Threshold Monitoring · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Alert on spend with HTTP / REST API and Slack end to end. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual real-time alerts is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Every Few Minutes → Fetch Current Metrics → Compute Severity Signals → Grade Severity Tier → Just Record (Info) → Merge Tier Outcomes → Send Summary to Slack

## Use it for
- Teams that want to threshold monitoring without manual effort
- Anyone automating real-time alerts in software development
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Responds in seconds
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Saves hours of manual work each week

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2 / service account; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Three response tiers, not a single yes/no alert, match the response to the severity — logging every metric quietly would bury the one critical spike, and paging on-call for every info-level blip trains them to ignore alerts entirely. Critical gets a distinct, louder channel from warning on purpose.

## Practice exercises

New to this pattern? Try these in order:

1. Adjust the tier thresholds and see how alert sensitivity changes.
2. Trigger a critical-tier value and confirm it pages a different channel than warning does.
3. Add a 4th tier between warning and critical.

## 🛟 When this fails

This starter workflow keeps things simple: no automatic retry or error branch yet. If a node fails, the execution just stops — open n8n's **Executions** tab to see which node and why. Ready to harden it? Turn on **Retry On Fail** (3 tries, 2s backoff) on the Settings tab of each external node, and add an error-output branch to a Slack/email alert — every Production-tier template in this library shows that exact pattern.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your real-time alerts process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
